title: He’s running his 11th Boston Marathon for Boston Children’s Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Steven Iannacone will run his 11th Boston Marathon supporting Boston Children's Hospital's Miles for Miracles program.
Iannacone's decade of running honors Jace Perkins, a former NICU baby born weighing 1.6 pounds, now a healthy 12-year-old.
Jace's survival was aided by Boston Children's Hospital NICU and Dr. Warf's team, inspiring Iannacone's continued support.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a quiz to test knowledge on recent Greater Boston news stories.
Peter Chianca has been Boston.com's general assignment editor since 2019.
The quiz aims to engage readers with trending stories in the Boston area.

title: Red Sox took advantage of MLB’s new automated strike challenge rules on Opening Day
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox defeated the Reds 3-0 on Opening Day, utilizing MLB's new Automated Ball-Strike Challenge System.
Roman Anthony's successful ninth-inning challenge reversed a critical call, leading to a walk and subsequent Red Sox scoring.
Each team begins with two ABS challenges, retained when successful; only batters, pitchers, or catchers can initiate challenges.

title: In first Opening Day with Red Sox, Marcelo Mayer made quite the impression off the bench
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marcelo Mayer impressed on Opening Day with two hits and two runs in a pinch-hit role against the Reds.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora delayed naming Mayer to the roster due to previous offensive struggles.
Boston's pitching limited Cincinnati to four hits, securing a 3-0 victory in their season opener.

title: Lucky pup thanks NH firefighters for quick thinking that saved his life
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Firefighters at Campton-Thornton Fire Department saved Ziggy, a dog, by stopping severe bleeding from a leg injury.
Ziggy's sitter sought help from firefighters after a leg wound worsened while heading to the vet last month.
Firefighters' quick tourniquet application prevented dangerous blood loss, allowing Ziggy to receive surgery successfully.

title: What’s causing my car to burn so much oil?
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2019 Subaru Legacy with 89,000 miles shows excessive oil consumption, potentially due to oil control piston rings.
Subaru suggests replacing the short block for around $4,000, but an engine flush and checking the PCV system might help.
A faulty PCV system can also cause oil consumption issues, making it important to explore before costly repairs.

title: This Nantucket diner just sold its fifth million dollar prize in the last 2 years
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Old South Diner on Nantucket sold its fifth million-dollar lottery ticket in two years, state lottery officials report.
The diner recently sold two $2 million lottery tickets, one on March 4 and another on March 26.
Old South Diner receives a $20,000 bonus for selling the latest winning ticket, as per the Massachusetts State Lottery Commission.

title: School bus driver in crash that killed child was allegedly involved in hit-and-run months later
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jean Charles, a former Boston school bus driver, was arraigned on charges related to a child's death in Hyde Park in April 2025.
Charles faces involuntary manslaughter and motor vehicle homicide charges after allegedly failing to ensure safe bus drop-offs.
Months later, Charles was involved in an alleged hit-and-run incident while driving with a suspended license in Brockton.

title: 5 takeaways as Garrett Crochet, Marcelo Mayer steer Red Sox to 3-0 Opening Day win over Reds
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Garrett Crochet struck out eight in six innings, helping the Red Sox achieve a 3-0 Opening Day victory over the Reds.
Marcelo Mayer's pinch-hit double sparked Boston's scoring in the seventh, assisting in their win against Cincinnati.
Roman Anthony contributed significantly, going 3 for 4, as the youngest Red Sox outfielder to start Opening Day since 1973.

title: Body of Mass. man missing since escaping from a hospital in 2025 found in lake
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Leonard Mercury's body was found in Crystal Lake, Gardner, five months after escaping from Heywood Hospital.
Mercury, 57, had been missing since October 31 after leaving a hospital in mental and medical distress.
The cause of his death is under investigation by the Office of the Chief Medical Examiner.
